text: When you're writing code, encountering bugs is normal. The first step is identifying the bug by observing unexpected behavior in your program. A systematic approach helps you solve problems efficiently: instead of guessing, reproduce the error, isolate the part of the code causing it, and test your fix. Debugging tools like breakpoints in an IDE let you pause execution and inspect variables step by step. Logging is another powerful strategy—printing messages at key points in your code shows you the flow of data and helps find where things go wrong. By combining these methods, you can resolve issues calmly and become a better programmer.
